acceptance_criteria: Blurhash matches reference TypeScript implementation output for test images; different images produce different hashes; stored as non-null string
description:
Compute blurhash for every uploaded image. Downscale to 32x32 (fast — any resampling filter OK at this size). Compute blurhash using the 'blurhash' crate. Store result in media.blurhash. Blurhash must be present on every MediaAttachment — Mastodon clients expect it.

AGENT WORKFLOW: Spawn a research subagent to find the reference blurhash implementation (TypeScript/Go) and generate reference blurhashes for 3 test images to use as oracle.

TEST DISCIPLINE:
- Compute blurhash for a known test image (e.g. all-red 4x4 pixel PNG)
- Compare against reference blurhash generated by the official TypeScript implementation for the same image
- Verify the output is a valid blurhash string (starts with 4-5 char header, correct length)
- Verify different images produce different blurhashes
- Do NOT test 'blurhash decodes back to same image' — that uses our own code as oracle

DEFENSIVE PROGRAMMING:
- Blurhash computation failure: store empty string, not null — MediaAttachment.blurhash must always be a string per Mastodon API
title: Blurhash: compute from 32x32 thumbnail, store in media row
